author: mohmyo
text: Pure JWT authentication class without tieing it to any user, not even TokenUser

author: mohmyo
text:
Hello,

I found myself yesterday working on a part which a token is only needed there just for the sake of anonymous access and to share some info in the payload. I already know the concepts of JWT and used it before but this is the first time for me to use it in DRF.

I only needed to create and validate a JWT token for only one endpoint which is different from the standard way which is using it as the authentication class for your whole system.

I dug a bit and found that you have to create your `access` token for `TokenUser` so authentication class passes it, but can't we also provide another authentication class which only authenticate for pure JWT token validity? without a user as it's not needed, not even the dummy one like `TokenUser`?

I find this very reasonable because some times all you want is just generating a JWT token and something that verifies it and gain access to your view when it's valid and otherwise not.

Thank you all for this great job.

author: Andrew-Chen-Wang
text: When I was initially testing out SimpleJWT with native Django 3.0 websockets, I ran into a similar issue of needing only one endpoint. Worse yet, database accessing is dangerous async (until psycopg3 comes out + Django ORM re-written). However, the SimpleJWT library is designed for authentication purposes. Especially since your use-case is for a single view, too, you should just use the `jwt` library which is pre-packaged in this library (as in pip install downloads simplejwt and jwt) and play around with it instead of using the simplejwt library to try anything out.
